# Source Triage and Language Guide

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

## Use precise language

| Evidence type | Recommended wording |
|---|---|
| Regulator action | “The ACCC alleged...” / “The FTC rule prohibits...” |
| Law-enforcement plea | “The DOJ said...” / “pleaded guilty to...” |
| Platform policy | “The platform prohibits...” |
| Journalism | “X reported...” |
| Contested case | “Reported allegations; the company denied...” |
| Industry artifact | “The agency/vendor describes...” |
| Reddit/forum | “Anecdotal operator chatter suggests...” |

## Avoid

- “Proves fraud” unless there is a legal finding or admission.
- “Everyone does this.”
- “The algorithm definitely...” unless supported by platform or academic evidence.
- Treating Reddit posts as factual proof.
- Mixing legal claims with ethical claims without distinction.

## Strong phrasing

- “This case illustrates the corruption of a credibility signal.”
- “The policy boundary is already visible; enforcement and attribution are the harder problems.”
- “The metric may be real but non-representative.”
- “The audience is denied context necessary to interpret the signal.”
